svg.title: HyperWeave receipt artifact
svg.desc: A receipt artifact, using primer genome.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: The same 800x578 economics proof on paper — cost outcome, working-token spend, the cost-by-model shelf and the context burn curve — seated on the README white by the two-layer primer drop shadow inside the 14px-radius card.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Identical solved geometry to the dark substrate (zones at y28/72/93, tool rows at the 23px pitch, shelf bar at y288, curve at y350): one substrate-invariant stencil. Light variants supply the paper-card gradient, the cobalt/variant signal and the dy2/dy6 lift drop-shadow, whose flood-color is a literal genome value because var() never resolves in filter attributes. The tool ramp and the area fill draw from the genome's receipt_ramp tiers, not chassis constants.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Kept one geometry across dark and light so measured == rendered coordinates cannot drift across the eight variants; substrate nuance lives in material tokens (paper gradient, lift, ink roles), never a second layout stencil. Semantic red steps to #DC2626 for AA on paper while staying genome-invariant — state is not skin. The burn curve is modelled per-stage from the compact payload, trading exact per-call occupancy for a disclosed summary.
